name: PyMuPDF (fitz)
what_it_does: Fast Python PDF/XPS/EPUB processing library that extracts text, images, annotations, and metadata from PDFs at 10-20x the speed of alternatives.
use_cases: Extract text from 1000-page PDF reports maintaining reading order and page coordinates
Convert PDF pages to high-resolution PNG images for vision model processing
Extract all embedded images from PDF documents for downstream image analysis
Search for specific text patterns across large PDF collections using page-by-page scan
Annotate PDFs programmatically — add highlights, redactions, watermarks, and bookmarks
not_for: Extracting tables with complex borders — use Camelot or Tabula for tabular data
Commercial applications without a commercial MuPDF license (AGPL license restriction)
Web browser environments — requires native MuPDF C library installation
best_when: High-throughput PDF text/image extraction where processing speed matters and AGPL license is acceptable.
avoid_when: You need a permissive license for commercial software — AGPL requires open-sourcing your code unless you purchase a commercial license.

auth.notes: Local Python library, no network auth

pricing.paid_tiers: Commercial license required for proprietary applications
pricing.notes: AGPL v3 — commercial use requires separate license from Artifex

performance.rate_limits: None — local library; 10-20x faster than pdfplumber for bulk extraction

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Exceptions are descriptive; encrypted PDF errors are clear; corrupted file handling varies
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent; page modifications require explicit save
known_agent_gotchas: AGPL license — embedding in proprietary software without commercial license is a legal violation; verify before deployment
Scanned PDFs return empty text — must combine with Tesseract/EasyOCR for image-based documents
fitz.open() does not raise on corrupted files — check doc.is_pdf and page count before processing
Text extraction order follows content streams, not visual reading order — complex multi-column layouts may require sort_coords=True
pip install pymupdf downloads pre-built wheels (300-500MB total); fails on platforms without wheel support and requires MuPDF build toolchain

security_score_components.security_notes: Process only trusted PDFs — malicious PDFs can exploit parser vulnerabilities; keep library updated
